what_it_does: A React class-component error boundary that wraps a named panel, catches render-time errors via getDerivedStateFromError, and renders a contained warning card so a single bad panel does not crash the whole canvas.
observations: Carved from App.tsx lines 7–17 according to the inline comment
Uses React class Component with getDerivedStateFromError
State is only a boolean err flag
Renders a div with className op-panel op-err when an error is caught
Error message tells the operator to revert the panel from the grow panel
Constructor parameter and children prop are typed as any
standouts: Per-panel containment rather than a top-level application error boundary
Inline comment explicitly links the component to a PRESERVE-LIST item and bridge-side recovery
conventions: TypeScript with loose any typing for constructor argument and children
concerns: Constructor parameter typed as any weakens type safety
children typed as any instead of ReactNode
No internal logging or reporting of the caught error
No reset mechanism; recovery is entirely external
notes: Small, focused per-panel error boundary that trades some type safety for quick containment of malformed panel definitions.
questions: What is the 'grow panel' and how does an operator revert a panel?
Is bridge-side recovery triggered automatically or manually?
Why is the constructor parameter typed as any?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: it catches render errors for a named panel and renders a contained error card instead of propagating the failure.
apparent_themes: Protection of the operator control surface
intention_signals: To prevent any single malformed or throwing panel from white-screening the canvas by isolating its failure behind a small, actionable error card.
contribution: Provides a localized error boundary primitive that keeps the rest of the canvas interactive when an individual panel fails to render.
summary_for_embedding: PanelErrorBoundary is a React class component that catches render errors in a named panel and displays a contained error card instead of crashing the canvas.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to isolate panel-level rendering failures so the operator's canvas control surface remains usable.

symbols.does: React class error boundary that catches render errors in a named panel and renders a fallback warning card.
symbols.description: Exported class component extending React.Component; it keeps an err boolean in state, derives that state from errors via getDerivedStateFromError, and either renders its children normally or a styled div instructing the operator to revert the failing panel from the grow panel.
